request.question:
Treating IMR-90 fibroblasts with 100 nM rapamycin for 72 hours will reduce the number of p21-positive senescent cells by at least 40% compared to vehicle controls following hydrogen peroxide-induced senescence, due to mTORC1 inhibition reactivating autophagy-mediated clearance of damaged organelles.
In plain English: can a drug already used in transplant patients slow or reverse cellular aging in a dish?

hypothesis.threshold: at least 40% compared to vehicle controls
hypothesis.mechanistic_rationale: mTORC1 inhibition reactivating autophagy-mediated clearance of damaged organelles
hypothesis.readiness_rationale: All core fields are present and specific; CRO-ready today.
hypothesis.literature_search_hint: rapamycin IMR-90 fibroblasts cellular aging p21 senescence
hypothesis.original_hypothesis:
Treating IMR-90 fibroblasts with 100 nM rapamycin for 72 hours will reduce the number of p21-positive senescent cells by at least 40% compared to vehicle controls following hydrogen peroxide-induced senescence, due to mTORC1 inhibition reactivating autophagy-mediated clearance of damaged organelles.
In plain English: can a drug already used in transplant patients slow or reverse cellular aging in a dish?

literature_qc.explanation: The retrieved papers do not specifically evaluate the intervention of 100 nM rapamycin on IMR-90 fibroblasts or directly measure the outcome of p21-positive senescent cells. While some papers discuss cellular senescence and the effects of rapamycin, none match the exact biological system or the specific intervention context required by the hypothesis.
literature_qc.recommended_action: No directly matching publication found. This combination appears novel. Proceed with experiment planning.

plan.objective: Determine the effect of 100 nM rapamycin on the number of p21-positive senescent IMR-90 fibroblast cells compared to vehicle controls.
plan.experimental_design: IMR-90 fibroblasts will be divided into two groups: a treatment group exposed to 100 nM rapamycin and a control group exposed to vehicle alone. After a defined incubation period, cells will be stained to quantify p21-positive senescent cells using flow cytometry.
controls: IMR-90 fibroblasts treated with 100 nM rapamycin for identified effects on senescence markers.
IMR-90 fibroblasts treated with the vehicle only, without rapamycin.

step_by_step_protocol.description:
DAY 0 (PREP) | 5 min | Cell Culture Preparation
Thaw IMR-90 fibroblast cells from cryopreservation in a warm 37°C water bath until fully thawed.
Expected outcome: Cells are thawed and viable after gentle transfer to culture medium without damage.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 24 h | Cell Culture Preparation
Resuspend thawed IMR-90 fibroblasts in 10 mL of DMEM supplemented with 10% FBS and 1% penicillin-streptomycin at 37°C with 5% CO2 for 24 h for recovery.
Expected outcome: Cells adhere to the culture flask and reach confluence.

DAY 1 | 10 min | Treatment Application
Wash cells twice with 5 mL of sterile PBS at room temperature to remove serum and antibiotics.
Expected outcome: Cells are adequately rinsed of serum and antibiotic residues.

DAY 1 | 5 min | Treatment Application
Add 6 mL of DMEM with either 100 nM rapamycin or vehicle control to respective groups of IMR-90 fibroblasts.
Expected outcome: Cells are treated with rapamycin or vehicle without loss.

DAY 1 | 72 h | Incubation
Incubate fibroblasts at 37°C with 5% CO2 for 72 h post-treatment to allow rapamycin action.
Expected outcome: Cells remain healthy and attached during incubation.

DAY 4 | 5 min | Sample Preparation for Flow Cytometry
Detach cells from culture flasks using 2 mL of TrypLE Express at 37°C for 5 min.
Expected outcome: Cells are successfully detached without damage.

DAY 4 | 10 min | Sample Preparation for Flow Cytometry
Resuspend detached cells in 5 mL of DMEM and centrifuge at 300 × g for 5 min at room temperature to pellet cells.
Expected outcome: Cell pellet is collected at the bottom of the tube.

DAY 4 | 30 min | Flow Cytometry
Stain cells with 50 µL of anti-p21 antibody (1:100 dilution in PBS) at room temperature for 30 min in the dark.
Expected outcome: Cells are stained and ready for analysis to quantify p21 expression.

DAY 4 | 1 h | Flow Cytometry
Analyze stained samples on the BD FACSCalibur utilizing appropriate settings to detect p21-positive cells.
Expected outcome: Flow cytometry data demonstrates an increase in p21-positive cells in rapamycin treatment group compared to vehicle.

DAY 4 | 30 min | Data Analysis
Calculate the percentage of p21-positive senescent cells in both groups and compare using a Student's t-test for statistical significance.
Expected outcome: Statistical analysis reveals whether the percentage of p21-positive cells is significantly greater than 40% in the rapamycin treatment group.

assumptions: IMR-90 fibroblasts will respond similarly to rapamycin as previously reported for other cell types in terms of senescence.
The concentration of rapamycin used (100 nM) is adequate to induce significant senescence responses in IMR-90 fibroblasts without cytotoxicity.
The staining protocol used for p21 is specific and sensitive enough to detect changes in senescence markers.
decision_criteria: Proceed if p21-positive cells are greater than 40% in the rapamycin treatment group compared to vehicle controls.
Proceed only if cell viability remains above 80% post-treatment as assessed by trypan blue exclusion test.
Terminate if there is excessive cytotoxicity observed in the rapamycin-treated group.
reproducibility_notes: Use of the same batch of FBS and DMEM for culturing cells to minimize variability.
Maintain consistent rapamycin preparation methods and handling to ensure proper dosage.
Standardize flow cytometry calibration and settings for cell analysis.

validation.primary_endpoint: Post-thaw viability percentage at immediate and 24h checkpoints.
validation.success_threshold: at least 40% compared to vehicle controls improvement over comparator in replicate-confirmed data.
failure_conditions: No meaningful viability gain in replicate-confirmed runs.
Intervention increases delayed viability loss at 24h.
validation.suggested_statistical_comparison: Two-group comparison against comparator using t-test or Mann-Whitney based on normality.
validation.minimum_replicates_or_design_note: Minimum three biological replicates before execution-level claim.

scope_of_work: Execute pilot-first HeLa cryopreservation comparison between DMSO baseline and trehalose arm.
Deliver replicate-confirmed viability analysis and mitigation traceability report.
cro_ready_brief.sample_count: At least 3 biological replicates per arm
